cross_scene_arcs.title: From Captive to Freed Prisoner
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jonson is captured after hiding manuscripts, imprisoned for years, and unexpectedly released by unknown allies.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Who arranged his release and whether it is legal

cross_scene_arcs.title: Custodian of Oxford's Secret
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jonson is coerced by Oxford to take credit for plays, later entrusted with protecting and publishing Oxford's manuscripts, which he retrieves after lying to authorities.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Overwhelmed and coerced in the maze
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Possesses the manuscripts and bears the responsibility
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: How to keep the secret while publishing the works

cross_scene_arcs.title: Professional Ban and Political Informing
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jonson refuses to endorse a manuscript, is banned from the Globe by Shakespeare, and then informs Lord Pole about Shakespeare's portrayal of Richard III.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Defending artistic integrity in the tavern
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Banned and acting as an informant
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The consequences of his informing on Shakespeare

cross_scene_arcs.title: Trauma of Witnessing Violence
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jonson discovers Marlowe's dead body and later witnesses Francesco's killing, leaving him shocked and horrified.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: How Jonson processes these violent events

cross_scene_arcs.title: Managing Secret Playwriting
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Oxford forces Jonson to publicly claim authorship of his plays, witnesses Shakespeare take credit on stage, and on his deathbed entrusts Jonson with preserving and publishing his manuscripts.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Forces Jonson to take credit for plays with money and threats
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Entrusts Jonson with the secret and manuscripts for posthumous publication

cross_scene_arcs.title: Conflict with Cecil from Youth to Revelation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Oxford's artistic interests are suppressed under Cecil's guardianship, leading to a forced marriage ultimatum, later political strategy to remove Cecil, and culminating in Cecil's revelation of Oxford's royal lineage and his son's condemnation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Denied poetry studies in Cecil's Puritan household
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Devastated by the revelation of his secret royal lineage and his son's impending execution
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: How to respond to his son's execution and the loss of his potential kingship

cross_scene_arcs.title: Relationship with Southampton and Legacy Condemnation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Oxford creates a rift with Southampton by refusing his blessing, later discovers Southampton is his son condemned to death, and pleads with Elizabeth to spare him, securing Southampton's survival but having his own plays and legacy disowned.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Refuses blessing to Southampton, creating a personal rift
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Secures Southampton's sparing after his death but leaves with his legacy condemned by the crown
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Oxford's legacy remains unrecognized and disowned

cross_scene_arcs.title: Struggle for Authorial Recognition
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Shakespeare's authorship is publicly doubted, but he claims it on stage, achieves celebrated success with Hamlet, and posthumously has his works dedicated by Jonson.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Authorship questioned and described as conjecture.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Works dedicated by Jonson, retired as grain merchant in Stratford.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Conflict and Rivalry with Ben Jonson
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jonson dismisses Shakespeare's acting ambitions, accuses him of murder, is banned from the Globe, confronts him physically, but later dedicates his collected works.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jonson commands Shakespeare to focus on writing and avoid acting.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jonson dedicates Shakespeare's collected works posthumously.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The murder accusation remains unaddressed in their direct interactions.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Patronage and Blackmail with Oxford
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Oxford is shocked by Shakespeare's claim of authorship, but Shakespeare blackmails him for increased payment, and Oxford continues to patronize him with orders and payments for secret performances.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Oxford realizes Shakespeare may be usurping his work.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Oxford pays Shakespeare and orders secret performances with short notice.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The blackmail threat and details of secret performances are unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rise from Actor to Gentleman Playwright
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Shakespeare transitions from aspiring actor to celebrated playwright, gains financial leverage through blackmail, publishes poetry, assumes authority in theater crises, and retires as a wealthy merchant.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Denied acting role by Jonson and told to act like a writer.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Retired from theater, living in Stratford as a grain merchant.
